citation_gate.rationale: 5% of 8 = 0.4 -> 0 uncited allowed at current size. Headroom auto-scales: at 20 turns, 5% = 1 noise turn permitted. Tightens when N is small, loosens proportionally as the suite grows. Revisit if observed climbs above 2%.

orchestrator_gate.rationale: Each orchestrator scenario is a deterministic substring + routing assertion against a cassette or a controlled live env. No noise budget; any failure is a real regression. If the suite ever returns flaky reds, raise to 12.5% (1/8) only with a recorded reason.

redteam_gate.rationale: Zero high/critical is non-negotiable. 10% overall allows 0 fails at N=8 (10% of 8 = 0.8). Same scaling logic as citation gate. Increase only after deliberate decision in .squad/decisions.md.

foundry_evaluators.rationale: 3.0 is Foundry's documented default 'pass' bar on the 1..5 scale. Lower thresholds make the gate noise; higher needs human-labeled groundedness data we don't have for a hackathon.

tool_routing.rationale: Routing is deterministic given a cassette and a strict assertion against `tool_calls`. Zero tolerance until the suite proves flaky in live mode.

recalibration_protocol: Run all four gates against the offline cassettes; record observed numbers.
If observed_pct == 0 and N >= 8, keep thresholds as-is.
If observed_pct > 0 from a deterministic regression, FIX the regression — don't loosen the threshold.
If observed_pct > 0 from genuine flakiness in live mode, set threshold = ceil((1/N)*100) and record the new entry in .squad/decisions.md.
When N doubles, re-check that the threshold still represents 'roughly 1 noise scenario'; tighten if necessary.
